game_name: Glory of Heracles III: Silence of the Gods

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Central narrative framing device; hero discovers memory loss at beginning
Unique mechanic allowing hero to perform actions other characters cannot (cliff jumping, fall damage negation)
Standard turn-based party mechanics; established since Heracles II
Time-flow system affecting world state and NPC availability; introduced in Heracles II, continued in III
Gradual introduction of mythological creatures and locations as world unfolds

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Narrative-driven with exploration punctuated by combat encounters; story progression tied to memory recovery creates steady tension gradient

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Immortality enables access to previously impassable areas (cliff jumps, fall-damage locations)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to party mechanics and amnesia mystery; immortality power grants exploration freedom
notable_spikes: Exploration of hazardous terrain requiring immortality

notable_level_patterns.description: Hero's unique immortal status creates environmental puzzles and optional paths unavailable to standard party members; cliff jumps and fall damage immunity enable vertical exploration
Time-flow system creates rhythmic changes to world state, NPC locations, and available encounters; establishes temporal pacing separate from combat
Game progression structured around hero recovering memories and learning about acquired powers; each discovery unlocks new mechanical and spatial possibilities

sources_used: Wikipedia - Glory of Heracles series overview
